steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Divide both sides of $-2|x-7| = -8x$ by $-2$: $$|x-7| = 4x$$ Since $|x-7| \ge 0$, we need $x \ge 0$. Solve both cases, then reject any that fail a check-back.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$-\frac{7}{3}$': Case $x \ge 7$: $x - 7 = 4x \Rightarrow x = -\frac{7}{3}$. But $-\frac{7}{3} < 0$, so $|x-7| \ne 4x$ — rejected. ✗
  • Choice B — '$\frac{7}{5}$': Case $x < 7$: $7 - x = 4x \Rightarrow 7 = 5x \Rightarrow x = \frac{7}{5}$. Check: $|\frac{7}{5} - 7| = \frac{28}{5}$ and $4 \cdot \frac{7}{5} = \frac{28}{5}$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$-\frac{7}{3}$ and $\frac{7}{5}$': Includes the rejected value. ✗
  • Choice D — '$\frac{7}{5}$ and $\frac{7}{3}$': $\frac{7}{3}$ comes from the $x \ge 7$ case solved incorrectly without rejecting. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
